category: stale meta description contradicting current page body

consistency.notes: All non-null/non-empty ground-truth fields are supported by the page text. Product title, roaster, location, origin, region, process, variety, producer/farm, altitude, harvest, roast, tasting notes, producer text, price, currency, bag size, package grams, one-time purchase context, and in-stock availability are explicitly shown. Boolean inferences also follow: not a blend (single-origin Colombia with one variety/producer), not espresso (explicitly roasted for filter rather than espresso), not decaf (no decaf indication), not coferment/infused (described as white honey processed coffee with no co-fermentation/infusion), and specialty coffee is supported by the detailed single-origin specialty-style product page.

realism.reasons:
Very plausible as a real roaster product page. The overall structure is right for a Shopify-style coffee PDP rendered to text: breadcrumb, product title, roast type, price, size selector, stock note, tasting notes, producer/origin/process/variety/altitude/harvest fields, descriptive copy, brew recommendations, shipping note, related products, variant block, footer. The coffee details are coherent and realistic: Pink Bourbon from Huila, white honey process, altitude range, named producer, harvest window, and flavor notes all fit specialty coffee conventions. The prose sounds natural and brand-appropriate rather than obviously machine-generated.

The main tell is the metadata mismatch: the meta description and og:title refer to a different coffee ('Kenya Gichathaini AA') while the visible page is for 'Colombia La Esperanza Pink Bourbon.' That kind of CMS/SEO carryover error can happen on real sites, but it is the strongest synthetic or assembled-text signal here. A smaller tell is that the visible option selector shows only '250g' while the embedded variant data includes both 250g and 1kg; again possible in a text extraction, but a slight inconsistency. Also, the page says '£22.00' without clarifying it is the default variant price despite the presence of a 1kg variant at £78.00, which is common on ecommerce pages but worth noting.

Overall: convincingly realistic with minor inconsistencies, especially in the meta tags.

label.coffee.producer_or_farm: María Fernanda Ospina, Finca La Esperanza
label.coffee.sensory_text:
Tasting Notes: Apricot jam, orange blossom, panela

In the cup we find apricot jam, orange blossom and a soft panela sweetness, with a silky body and a clean citrus finish.
label.coffee.producer_text:
This lot is a sparkling Pink Bourbon from Finca La Esperanza in Palestina, Huila. María Fernanda Ospina and her family have been refining their picking and drying protocols for small separated lots, and this white honey process keeps the cup intensely sweet while showing the florals we love in this variety.

Ripe cherries are hand sorted, depulped with a thin layer of mucilage left intact, then dried slowly on raised beds under shade for 18-22 days.
